prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response: The lighthouse keeper, a solemn man, had been responsible for guiding ships through the treacherous waters for many years. He was used to the solitude, the monotony of his task, the rhythmic rotation of the lamp. But on this particular night, a dense fog rolled in, unusual in its thickness. As he tended to his duties, he noticed a peculiar object in the distance, barely visible through the haze. Curiosity piqued, he grabbed his spyglass and made his way to the highest point of the lighthouse. Peering through the lens, he was shocked to see what appeared to be a small, metallic structure bobbing in the water. It seemed to be pulsing with an ethereal light, almost reminiscent of the stars in the night sky. Intrigued, he watched as it moved steadily towards the shore, wondering what secrets this unexpected visitor might bring with it.
